betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Assembler§f:
The Assembler (nuclearcraft:assembler) is a single-block machine that combines multiple components into complex products. It is the NC equivalent of a vanilla Crafting Table for advanced recipes.

§6Slot Layout (AssemblerRecipes.java)§f:
The Assembler accepts up to 6 item inputs (1 + 5) and produces 1 item output. It is the only NC machine that supports multi-input assembly recipes.

§6Crafting Recipe (CraftingRecipeHandler.java:94)§f:
Pattern "PHP / ACA / PMP" in a 3×3 grid. Letters: P = plateBasic, H = ingotHardCarbon, A = actuator, C = chassis, M = motor. Yields 1 Assembler.

§6What It Does (AssemblerRecipes.java)§f:
The Assembler handles:
- §bNC part crafting§f: chassis, motor, actuator, servo, solenoidCopper, solenoidMagnesiumDiboride, etc.
- §bPlate crafting§f: plateBasic, plateAdvanced, plateDU, plateElite
- §bUpgrade module crafting§f: Speed Upgrade, Energy Upgrade
- §bWire crafting§f: Magnesium Diboride Wire, BSCCO Wire
- §bComponent assembly§f: Lithium Ion Cells, Machine Frames, Bioplastic, etc.
- §bVanilla-style complex crafts§f: any recipe with 3+ ingredients that doesn't fit the Manufactory pattern

§6Direct Descendants of Assembler (TaskRetrieval)§f:
The graph shows 4 items used in the Assembler recipe:
- chassis (nuclearcraft:part:10) — Machine Chassis
- plateAdvanced (nuclearcraft:part:1)
- plateBasic (nuclearcraft:part:0)
- ingotEuphemium (hbm) — HBM NTM material, used as a catalyst

§6Why It Matters§f:
The Assembler is the §eworkhorse of mid-game NC progression§f. Almost every NC component (parts, plates, machines, upgrades) is built in the Assembler. It is the bottleneck for scaling up any NC production line.

§6Assembling Speed (AssemblerRecipes)§f:
The Assembler's default cycle time is 600 ticks (30 seconds). With Speed Upgrades:
- 1 Speed Upgrade: 300 ticks (15 sec)
- 2 Speed Upgrades: 150 ticks (7.5 sec)
- 3 Speed Upgrades: 75 ticks (3.75 sec)

Power consumption scales with speed.

§6Prerequisites§f:
Requires q30021 (Alloy Furnace) — the basic NC unlock.

§eTip§f: Build a dedicated "assembly line" of 4-8 Assemblors with speed upgrades. Almost every component you'll need downstream (motors, solenoids, plates, chassis) is built here.

§eTip§f: Use an ME Interface or Item Conduit to auto-supply the Assemblers with materials. Each cycle requires multiple ingredients, and manual feeding becomes tedious at scale.

§cWarning§f: The Assembler is the largest RF consumer in the early NC tech tree. Make sure your power infrastructure (Voltaic Pile + battery bank) is sized accordingly.
